question: Will CSCO's market close price on 2026-06-28 be higher than its market close price on 2026-06-21? Stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for in resolving this question. Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price. Resolution criteria: Resolves to the market close price at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/CSCO for the resolution date. If the resolution date coincides with a day the market is closed (weekend, holiday, etc.) the previous market close price is used. Background: Cisco Systems, Inc. designs, develops, and sells technologies that help to power, secure, and draw insights from the internet in the Americas, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, the Asia Pacific, Japan, and China. The company offers data center switching; network security, identity and access management, and secure access service edge; threat intelligence, detection, and response solutions; interconnects public and private wireline and mobile networks, delivering connectivity to campus, data center, and branch networks; WEBEX suite, collaboration devices, and contact center; communication platform as a service software, including perpetual licenses, subscription arrangements, and hardware solutions; network assurance, monitoring and analytics, and observability suite; issue resolution, software support, and hardware replacement; professional services, such as planning, design, implementation, and high-value consulting; service and support packages, financing, and managed network services; and regional, national, and international wireline carriers, webscale products, internet, and cable. It also delivers connectivity to campus, data center, and branch networks; wireless products, including indoor and outdoor wireless coverage designed for seamless roaming use of voice, video, and data applications; end-to-end collaboration solutions through cloud, on-premise, or within hybrid cloud environments, transition collaboration solutions from on-premise to the cloud; and network assurance, monitoring and analytics, and observability suite. In addition, it offers technical support and advisory services. The company serves businesses, public institutions, governments, and service providers. It sells its products and services directly, through systems integrators, service providers, resellers, and distributors. Cisco Systems, Inc. was incorporated in 1984 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

evidences.title: Cisco Systems (CSCO) Stock Forecast: Analyst Ratings, Predictions & Price Target 2026
evidences.content: Buy 40% Hold 13% Sell 0% Strong Sell 0% #### Bulls say Cisco Systems is the leading provider of networking equipment and software, with a strong market share in both segments. Its growing demand for AI infrastructure and a strong backlog of orders suggest potential for continued double-digit growth. The company is also expanding its foothold in the cybersecurity market, with strong order growth in its core security portfolio expected to continue. Despite potential challenges, Cisco's well-managed expenses and stable gross margins support a positive outlook for its financial performance in the near future. #### Bears say [...] Cisco Systems is facing headwinds in its service provider and cloud business due to slower growth in Telco orders, while also struggling with supply chain challenges that could further impact revenue and gross margins. Despite solid order growth in its campus networking and wireless business, there is concern over the company's dependence on a broad portfolio of products and the need to continue developing new products for customer adoption. Additionally, the company's recent announcement of a workforce reduction may not be solely for cost-cutting and could...

evidences.title: CSCO Investor Relations - Cisco Systems Inc - Alpha Spread
evidences.content: Charles H. Robbins, commonly known as Chuck Robbins, is the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of Cisco Systems Inc., a global leader in networking technology. He joined the company in 1997 and has held various leadership roles, including Senior Vice President of the Americas and Senior Vice President of Worldwide Field Operations. Under Robbins' leadership, Cisco has focused on transforming its business toward software and subscription-based models, emphasizing cybersecurity, cloud computing, and the Internet of Things (IoT). He became CEO in July 2015, succeeding John Chambers, and later took on the role of Chairman of the Board in December 2017. [...] Deborah L. Stahlkopf is an accomplished legal professional serving as the Executive Vice President and Chief Legal Officer at Cisco Systems, Inc. She plays a critical role in overseeing Cisco's global legal affairs, guiding the company on a wide range of legal, compliance, and regulatory matters. With a rich background in both corporate law and public service, Stahlkopf brings a wealth of experience to her role at Cisco. [...] M. evidences.title: Cisco Systems, Inc. (CSCO) Future Performance Analysis (2026)
evidences.content: The primary growth drivers for a company like Cisco are transitioning its vast customer base from hardware-centric purchases to recurring software and subscription models. This shift improves revenue predictability and margins. Key opportunities lie in cross-selling security and observability solutions (from the Splunk acquisition) into its networking installed base. Market demand is fueled by long-term trends like AI, hybrid work, cybersecurity, and cloud adoption. However, Cisco's ability to capture this demand depends on its innovation pipeline and its success in competing against more specialized, agile competitors who are often perceived as technology leaders in these high-growth areas. [...] In the near term, a normal 1-year scenario for FY2025 projects `Revenue growth: ~+2% (analyst consensus)` and `EPS growth: ~+3% (analyst consensus)`, driven by stabilizing hardware demand and software growth. A bull case could see `Revenue growth: +5%` if Splunk integration yields rapid cross-selling synergies. A bear case, triggered by a deeper economic slowdown, could see `Revenue growth: -3%`. Over a 3-year period to FY2028, a normal scenario forecasts `Revenue CAGR: +3%` and `EPS C...

evidences.title: CSCO Stock Dividend: A Guide to Cisco’s Payout History
evidences.content: ## Key Dividend Dates ### Ex-Dividend Date To receive the CSCO stock dividend, investors must own the shares before the ex-dividend date. If you purchase the stock on or after this date, the upcoming dividend payment goes to the seller. Typically, Cisco’s ex-dividend dates fall in the months of January, April, July, and October. ### Declaration and Payment Dates The Board of Directors usually declares the dividend several weeks before the actual payment. Based on historical patterns, payments are distributed quarterly. For instance, recent filings indicate payment dates often occurring in late January or early February for the first fiscal quarter. ## Comparison with Industry Peers [...] daily\_trading\_volume\_value market\_share58.51% Current ETH GAS: 0.1-1 gwei Hot BTC ETF: IBIT Bitcoin Rainbow Chart : Accumulate Bitcoin halving: 4th in 2024, 5th in 2028 BTC/USDT$ (0.00%) index27(fear) banner.title:0(index.bitcoin) coin\_price.total\_bitcoin\_net\_flow\_value0 new\_userclaim\_now download\_appdownload\_now Bitget/ Crypto Wiki/ CSCO Stock Dividend: A Guide to Cisco’s Payout History # CSCO Stock Dividend: A Guide to Cisco’s Payout History Explore the CSCO stock dividend profile...

evidences.title: Cisco rises as networking equipment demand rebound takes root
evidences.content: Advertisement · Scroll to continue "After the past couple of quarters of meaningful inventory digestion headwinds, we viewed these order numbers as a positive," Morgan Stanley analysts said in a note. Cisco on Wednesday forecast fourth-quarter revenue between $13.4 billion and $13.6 billion, compared with analysts' estimates of $13.23 billion, according to LSEG data. "We currently expect customers to complete the installation of the majority of their inventory by the end of our fiscal year in July," said CEO Charles Robbins on a post-earnings call. Product orders were flat in the third quarter, excluding the impact of the Splunk buyout, compared with a decline of 12% in the previous quarter. evidences.title: Why Is Cisco (CSCO) Up 5.5% Since Last Earnings Report?
evidences.content: ### CSCO Sees Mixed Trends Outside Core Networking Security revenues were $2 billion, essentially flat year over year, as gains in newer offerings continued to be weighed down by declines in prior-generation products. Management noted progress in refreshed security products and highlighted that over 1,000 new customers purchased products such as Secure Access, XDR, Hypershield and AI Defense in the quarter, bringing net new customers since launch to roughly 5,000. At the same time, Cisco reiterated that Splunk's transition toward cloud subscriptions from on-premise deals is creating a near-term revenue drag, even as the company targets more than 1,000 new Splunk customer logos in fiscal 2026. Observability revenues were $269 million, up 3% year over year. [...] ## How Have Estimates Been Moving Since Then? In the past month, investors have witnessed a upward trend in fresh estimates. The consensus estimate has shifted 7.44% due to these changes. ## VGM Scores Currently, Cisco has a subpar Growth Score of D, however its Momentum Score is doing a lot better with an A. However, the stock has a score of F on the value side, putting it in the bottom 20% quintile for this investment s...

evidences.content: ## Future Outlook Looking ahead, Cisco’s future seems geared towards capitalizing on the growing demands for advanced networking solutions, cloud technologies, and cybersecurity. The company is well-positioned to benefit from trends such as the increasing adoption of smart devices, the expansion of cloud computing, and the widespread need for enhanced cybersecurity measures. Additionally, Cisco’s initiatives in 5G technology could open up new opportunities in the telecommunications sector. Cisco’s commitment to research and development is likely to drive future growth as it continues to innovate in the field of networking technology. The company is also expected to play a significant role in the development of smart cities and the next generation of Internet protocols. ## Learn More
